2604.13776.title: Who Gets Flagged? The Pluralistic Evaluation Gap in AI Content Watermarking
2604.13776.paper_summary: Watermarking is becoming the default mechanism for AI content authentication, with governance policies and frameworks referencing it as infrastructure for content provenance. Yet across text, image, and audio modalities, watermark signal strength, detectability, and robustness depend on statistical properties of the content itself, properties that vary systematically across languages, cultural visual traditions, and demographic groups. We examine how this content dependence creates modality-specific pathways to bias. Reviewing the major watermarking benchmarks across modalities, we find that, with one exception, none report performance across languages, cultural content types, or population groups. To address this, we propose three concrete evaluation dimensions for pluralistic watermark benchmarking: cross-lingual detection parity, culturally diverse content coverage, and demographic disaggregation of detection metrics. We connect these to the governance frameworks currently mandating watermarking deployment and show that watermarking is held to a lower fairness standard than the generative systems it is meant to govern. Our position is that evaluation must precede deployment, and that the same bias auditing requirements applied to AI models should extend to the verification layer.
2604.13776.paper_summary_zh: 水印技術正逐漸成為人工智慧內容認證的默認機制，治理政策和框架將其視為內容來源的基礎設施。<lb/>然而，在文本、圖像和音頻模式中，水印信號的強度、可檢測性和穩健性取決於內容本身的統計特性，這些特性在不同語言、文化視覺傳統和人口群體之間系統性地變化。<lb/>我們檢視這種內容依賴性如何創造出特定於模式的偏見途徑。<lb/>回顧各模式中的主要水印基準，我們發現，除了其中一個例外，沒有任何報告在語言、文化內容類型或人口群體之間的表現。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了三個具體的評估維度，用於多元水印基準測試：跨語言檢測平等、文化多樣內容覆蓋以及檢測指標的人口分解。<lb/>我們將這些與目前要求水印部署的治理框架聯繫起來，並顯示水印技術所遵循的公平標準低於其所應治理的生成系統。<lb/>我們的立場是，評估必須先於部署，並且應用於人工智慧模型的偏見審核要求應擴展到驗證層。
2604.13776.author: Alexander Nemecek et.al.
2604.13776.authors: Alexander Nemecek, Osama Zafar, Yuqiao Xu, Wenbiao Li, Erman Ayday
